content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
ROOM 3082
STATE CAPITOL
TELEPHONE: (916) 445-7807

COMMITTEES:

CHAIRMAN
HOUSING & URBAN AFFAIRS
SUBCOMMITTEE ON WOMEN IN THE
WORKFORCE
JOINT COMMITTEE ON
SCHOOL FACILITIES

MEMBER:
APPROPRIATIONS
BANKING & COMMERCE
BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS
EDUCATION
ENERGY & PUBLIC UTILITIES
INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS
TRANSPORTATION
JOINT COMMITTEE ON RULES

Senate
California Legislature

CAUCUS:
STATE ALLOCATION BOARD

LEROY F. GREENE
MEMBER OF CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE
SIXTH SENATE DISTRICT, SACRAMENTO COUNTY

MAJORITY CAUCUS-VICE-CHAIRMAN

May 16, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attention: Legal Department

Gentlemen:

I need a ruling. I have authored approximately 1300 newspaper
columns (copies available) over more than 25 years. These
columns have appeared in many newspapers throughout the state of
California. I am syndicated by Capitol News Service. No payment
has been sought or received for any of these efforts.

I now seek to expand my efforts to include magazines and seek
payment. The California Journal will carry one of my pieces in
the June 1991 issue. Additionally, I wrote a series of
children's fairy tales for my daughter many years ago that I now
intend to attempt to market.

What is my status? Am I a writer permitted payment for my
efforts? Are payments for my writing honoraria? If classified
as honoraria, am I prevented from ever being recognized as a
professional writer? How many payments for articles must I
receive and turn over to the State before being recognized as a
professional writer?

If I wrote only fairy tales and nothing else for payment (having
zero relationship with my role as a legislator) how does this
relate to the question of honoraria?

Very truly yours,

Leroy F. Greene

LEROY F. GREENE

LFG:gi

"REMEMBER — IF YOU ARE TO BE PROPERLY REPRESENTED, YOUR VIEWS MUST BE KNOWN"

sections.question: What is my status? Am I a writer permitted payment for my efforts? Are payments for my writing honoraria? If classified as honoraria, am I prevented from ever being recognized as a professional writer? How many payments for articles must I receive and turn over to the State before being recognized as a professional writer? If I wrote only fairy tales and nothing else for payment (having zero relationship with my role as a legislator) how does this relate to the question of honoraria?
sections.question_synthetic: A state legislator seeks clarification on whether payments for his newspaper columns, magazine articles, and children's fairy tales constitute honoraria under California law, and what steps are necessary to establish himself as a professional writer exempt from honoraria restrictions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a legislator to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document contains formatting issues and some OCR errors (e.g., 'Fsir Pc1:-t:-caL' appears to be 'Fair Political'). The letter contains multiple related questions about honoraria classification and professional writer status.

embedding.summary: State Senator Leroy F. Greene requests a ruling from the FPPC regarding his status as a writer, specifically whether payments he seeks for newspaper columns, magazine articles, and fairy tales would be classified as honoraria and whether this would prevent him from being recognized as a professional writer.
